reasoning: The flagged unsafe script (linux-setup-locales.sh) is not executed during makepkg/build/install processes and resides in resources/ directory unused by PKGBUILD. All build-related scripts reviewed are legitimate with no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged linux-setup-locales.sh as unsafe due to sudo usage, despite it being an unused development script irrelevant to AUR build/install.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing .install hooks (none present)
Qwen prioritized resources/linux-setup-locales.sh over core build scripts like prepare()/build() functions
false_positives: Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict on linux-setup-locales.sh: Script is inert during AUR build/install and exists only for developer convenience
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Weight PKGBUILD-invoked scripts higher than unrelated resources/ files

Add detection for build-execution context: Auto-flag if PKGBUILD references questionable paths
